cowardice_flags.avoided: Frames rural-urban gap as 'stubborn' due to generic 'connectivity, literacy, trust' without naming structural realities
cowardice_flags.should_say: Persistent cash dependency hits Malay-majority rural heartlands and East Malaysia hardest, rooted in informal cash-based economies and cultural preference for tangible transactions

cowardice_flags.avoided: Mature card treats mandatory interoperability as 'real trade-off' that might reduce innovation
cowardice_flags.should_say: BNM must force full DuitNow QR interoperability — current fragmentation is unsustainable taxpayer-subsidised inefficiency benefiting oligopolists while exploiting users

false_balance: Mature card pretends symmetry between consumer harm and hypothetical 'competitive pressure' when evidence demands decisive consolidation
audience_check: Real Malaysians would tune out — reads like sanitised BNM/policy brief lecturing urban elites while ignoring daily reality: cash still rules wet markets, e-wallet scams drain wages, 'convenience' means giving data to Grab/TNG.

hard_truths_missing.truth: Rural/semi-urban Malay populations lag in adoption from informal cash economies in agriculture/small trade and lingering distrust of urban-elite-dominated finance
Urban Chinese businesses capture outsized cashless benefits through higher e-commerce integration, reinforcing economic advantages
BNM and successive governments push cashless metrics for modernisation headlines and data leverage while subsidising unviable operators tied to politically connected conglomerates
hard_truths_missing.why_avoided: Direct critique of regulators violates 'considered view' neutrality

gender_truths_avoided.truth: Women in rural/lower-income face lower personal smartphone ownership, patriarchal financial autonomy limits, higher vulnerability to scams
gender_truths_avoided.why_uncomfortable: Exposes gendered power dynamics that cut across race-neutral narrative

class_truths_avoided.truth: Rural-urban and cash-vs-digital split is fundamentally a class issue of informal-sector workers (cross-ethnic) needing cash flexibility — 30-40% of economy is informal
class_truths_avoided.why_uncomfortable: Shifts blame from race/religion to Malaysia's shadow economy

predictions.if_nothing_changes: By 2027-2028, top 3 exceed 90% share; fees rise after subsidies end; rural cash drops to ~20% leaving millions excluded; data monetisation fuels discriminatory credit scoring
predictions.who_can_fix: BNM (mandatory interoperability, stricter licensing, real consent enforcement) and MOF (ending blanket subsidies)
predictions.why_they_wont: Political credit for 'cashless success' outweighs fixing pain points; dominant players have deep regulatory and business ties; rocking the boat risks digital-economy narrative

strongest_card.why: Names lived daily reality of rural-urban gap that rest of piece glosses over

weakest_card.why: Peak diplomatic cowardice — sees fire but refuses to say 'put it out'

overall_assessment: Polite hedged policy-speak. Spots fragmentation, bleeding operators, rural exclusion, data harvesting but refuses to connect to Malaysia's ethnic-class fractures or political incentives. Zero courage, performative neutrality, audience would dismiss as elite noise.
